_comment: Non-secret settings. Edit freely. Secrets (Gmail credentials) come from environment variables / GitHub secrets, NOT this file.
_followupIntervalDays: Wait at least this many days after your last message before following up.
_maxFollowups: How many follow-ups to send per professor before giving up. 1 = a single nudge.
_lookbackDays: Only consider emails you sent within this many days. Avoids nagging ancient threads.
_onlyThreadsIInitiated: If true, only follow up on threads where YOU sent the first message.
_allowedDomains: Only follow up with recipients whose email domain ENDS WITH or CONTAINS one of these. Empty list [] = allow all domains. This is your main safety net against emailing non-professors.
_skipDomains: Never follow up with these recipient domains, even if allowedDomains is empty.
_skipAddresses: Specific email addresses to always skip, e.g. someone you already heard from by phone.
_gmailLabel: Set to "outreach" so this follow-up tool chases ONLY the first-contact emails that outreach.js sent and labeled — not coursework, advisors, or admin mail. Must match outreach-config.json's gmailLabel. Empty = scan all sent threads instead.
_subjectMustInclude: If non-empty, the original subject must contain at least ONE of these words/phrases (case-insensitive) to qualify. Use to target outreach, e.g. ["PhD", "research", "prospective", "position", "opportunity"]. Empty = no include requirement.
_subjectMustExclude: If the original subject contains any of these (case-insensitive), the thread is skipped. Keeps routine academic mail (coursework, admin) from getting follow-ups.
_subjectPrefix: Prefix for the follow-up subject (kept as a reply).
_dryRun: TRUE = only log what WOULD be sent (no emails go out). Set to false once you've reviewed a dry run. Can be overridden by the DRY_RUN env var.
